_comment: My Little Pony franchise data file. All five toy generations, all TV/film/game media.
description: Hasbro's pony toy-and-media empire spanning five generations (1982–present). G1 set a dark-fantasy tone; G4 Friendship Is Magic (2010–2019) defined the franchise culturally, attracting an unexpected adult fandom (bronies). G5 launched 2021 and was discontinued in 2024.

media.title: My Little Pony: Rescue at Midnight Castle
media.description: First MLP animated special. Villain Tirac and the Rainbow of Darkness. Dark fantasy tone. 30 min.
media.notes: G1. TV special — functionally a film. Produced by Sunbow/Marvel, animated by Toei.

media.title: My Little Pony: Escape from Catrina
media.description: Second G1 animated special. Catrina the witch and her potion addiction allegory. 30 min.
media.notes: G1. TV special — functionally a film.

media.title: My Little Pony: The Movie
media.description: G1 theatrical film. Musical adventure vs the Smooze. Danny DeVito, Madeline Kahn, Rhea Perlman.
media.notes: G1. 421-theater limited release. Grossed $5M+.

media.title: My Little Pony 'n Friends
media.description: G1 main anthology series. Incorporates both 1984/1985 specials as two-parters. Human characters Megan, Danny, Molly.
media.notes: G1. Produced by Sunbow/Marvel, animated by Toei and AKOM.

media.title: My Little Pony Tales
media.description: G1 reboot. Slice-of-life tone, different cast. School-based setting. No human characters.
media.notes: G1. Divisive — significantly different tone from 'n Friends.

media.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Gardens
media.description: G2 virtual pet CD-ROM. Groom, feed, and play with a pony at the farm and village. The only G2 media release.
media.notes: G2. Published by Hasbro Interactive. DOSBox / Win98 VM to run today.

media.title: My Little Pony: Best Friends Ball
media.description: G3 PC activity game. Prepare four ponies for the Friendship Ball. Mini-games, dress-up, dance studio.
media.notes: G3. CD-ROM. Windows XP era.

media.title: My Little Pony: A Very Minty Christmas
media.description: G3 holiday DTV. Minty accidentally breaks the Here Comes Christmas Candy Cane. Most beloved G3 DTV.
media.notes: G3. Direct-to-video.

media.title: My Little Pony: The Princess Promenade
media.description: G3 spring DTV. Wysteria becomes a princess. Breezie friends feature prominently.
media.notes: G3. Direct-to-video.

media.title: My Little Pony Crystal Princess: The Runaway Rainbow
media.description: G3 DTV. Rarity teleports to Breezie Blossom and must return in time for the first rainbow of the season.
media.notes: G3. Tie-in to GBA game of the same name.

media.title: My Little Pony Crystal Princess: The Runaway Rainbow
media.description: G3 GBA platformer/adventure. Multi-pony playable cast. Best console MLP game. Emulatable via mGBA on mobile.
media.notes: G3. Best game in the franchise. mGBA core on RetroArch / EmulatorJS.

media.title: My Little Pony: Pinkie Pie's Party Parade
media.description: G3 DS mini-game collection. 10 games for Pinkie Pie's birthday party parade. Touch screen and microphone features.
media.notes: G3. melonDS compatible.

media.title: My Little Pony: Pinkie Pie's Birthday
media.description: G3 DS game. Find Sweetie Bell's hidden presents across Ponyville. 8 stylus-based mini-games.
media.notes: G3. melonDS compatible. Microphone gimmicks partially incompatible with emulation.

media.title: My Little Pony: Twinkle Wish Adventure
media.description: Final G3 DTV. A wishing star is granted to Cheerilee. G3 era closer.
media.notes: G3. Weakest of the G3 DTVs.

media.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic
media.description: G4 main series. Lauren Faust. Mane Six — Twilight Sparkle, Rainbow Dash, Rarity, Fluttershy, Applejack, Pinkie Pie. The franchise peak.
media.notes: G4. 2010–2019. Produced by DHX Media Vancouver. Brony fandom phenomenon.

media.title: My Little Pony: Magic Princess (Gameloft)
media.description: G4 mobile city-builder. Rebuild Ponyville, collect 300+ characters, official voice cast. Free-to-play with IAP. Still live.
media.notes: G4. Best MLP mobile game. Still actively updated as of 2026.

media.title: My Little Pony: Equestria Girls
media.description: First Equestria Girls film. Twilight crosses to Canterlot High. Sunset Shimmer introduced as antagonist.
media.notes: G4 spin-off. Anthro/human aesthetic. Limited theatrical run.

media.title: Equestria Girls App
media.description: G4 EG spin-off mobile game. Canterlot High fashion/social quests. Toy-scanning features. DEFUNCT.
media.notes: Removed from app stores. No longer playable.

media.title: My Little Pony: Equestria Girls — Rainbow Rocks
media.description: Best Equestria Girls film. The Dazzlings (siren villains) battle of the bands. Strong musical numbers.
media.notes: G4 spin-off. Direct-to-video.

media.title: My Little Pony: Twilight Sparkle, Teacher for a Day
media.description: G4 edutainment mobile game. Classroom/teaching mini-game theme. DEFUNCT — removed from stores.
media.notes: Cancelled. No longer available on any store.

media.title: My Little Pony: Equestria Girls — Friendship Games
media.description: Crystal Prep vs Canterlot High. Sci-Twi introduced. Magic leaks into the human world.
media.notes: G4 spin-off. Direct-to-video.

media.title: My Little Pony: Harmony Quest
media.description: G4 2D platformer. Mane Six fight a changeling invasion led by Queen Chrysalis. Unique abilities per pony.
media.notes: G4. Still available on app stores.

media.title: My Little Pony: AR Guide
media.description: G4 augmented reality app. Place Mane Six in real world, dual-device mode. DEFUNCT.
media.notes: Removed from Google Play 2021, removed from App Store ~2023.

media.title: My Little Pony: Equestria Girls — Legend of Everfree
media.description: Camp Everfree setting. Earth magic theme. Lighter tone. First EG Netflix exclusive.
media.notes: G4 spin-off. Netflix.

media.title: My Little Pony: The Movie
media.description: G4 theatrical film. Storm King invades Equestria. Emily Blunt, Taye Diggs, Zoe Saldana, Liev Schreiber. $6M budget, $60M gross.
media.notes: G4. Watch between FiM S7 and S8.

media.title: My Little Pony: Rainbow Runners
media.description: G4 auto-runner. Mane Six dodge obstacles to restore color to Ponyville after a Discord mishap.
media.notes: G4. Still available on app stores.

media.title: MLP Equestria Girls: Forgotten Friendship
media.description: Sunset's memories are erased by Wallflower Blush. Strong character writing. Best post-film EG entry.
media.notes: G4 EG spin-off. Discovery Family special. 44 min.

media.title: MLP Equestria Girls: Rollercoaster of Friendship
media.description: Theme park adventure. Vignette and Juniper as antagonists.
media.notes: G4 EG spin-off. Discovery Family special. 44 min.

media.title: My Little Pony: Best Gift Ever
media.description: G4 holiday special. Mane Six each try to find the perfect Hearth's Warming gift. Canon.
media.notes: G4. 44-min special. Functionally a holiday film.

media.title: MLP Equestria Girls: Spring Breakdown
media.description: Cruise ship magic threat. Rainbow Dash focus. Portal to Equestria reopens unexpectedly.
media.notes: G4 EG spin-off. Discovery Family special. 44 min.

media.title: MLP Equestria Girls: Sunset's Backstage Pass
media.description: Time loop at a music festival. Final Equestria Girls special. Closes Sunset Shimmer's arc.
media.notes: G4 EG spin-off. Discovery Family special. 44 min.

media.title: My Little Pony: Rainbow Roadtrip
media.description: G4 special. Mane Six travel to Hope Hollow, where the town has been drained of color. Mystery.
media.notes: G4. 60-min special. Functionally a film.

media.title: My Little Pony: School of Friendship
media.description: G4 arcade game. Pocket Pony Championship at the School of Friendship. DEFUNCT.
media.notes: Discontinued. No longer on app stores.

media.title: My Little Pony: Pony Life
media.description: G4 chibi comedy spin-off. Shorter episodes, slapstick tone. Same characters, very different style.
media.notes: G4 spin-off. Divisive. Not required viewing.

media.title: My Little Pony: Color By Magic
media.description: G4 coloring game. Restore the Museum of Friendship by coloring FiM illustrations. Relaxed gameplay.
media.notes: G4. Still available on app stores.

media.title: My Little Pony: A New Generation
media.description: G5 Netflix launch film. Sunny Starscout leads new Mane 5. Equestria's three pony tribes have forgotten each other. 3D CGI.
media.notes: G5. Boulder Media / Entertainment One. Best G5 entry.

media.title: My Little Pony: Make Your Mark
media.description: G5 main series. Continues from A New Generation. Introduces Misty Brightdawn. Ended November 2023.
media.notes: G5. DISCONTINUED with the generation in 2024.

media.title: My Little Pony: Tell Your Tale
media.description: G5 short-form series. 5-minute episodes. Supplementary slice-of-life. Ended October 2024.
media.notes: G5. DISCONTINUED. Light supplementary content.

media.title: My Little Pony: A Maretime Bay Adventure
media.description: G5 platformer. Sunny Starscout hosts Maretime Bay Day. Fashion shows, animal-finding, flying mini-games.
media.notes: G5. PC via Sunshine streaming. Requires modern hardware.

media.title: My Little Pony: Mane Merge
media.description: G5 Apple Arcade exclusive. Match-3 puzzle game with mini-games. Make Your Mark characters.
media.notes: G5. Apple Arcade subscription required. iOS and macOS only.

media.title: My Little Pony: World
media.description: G5 open-world mobile game. Explore Maretime Bay, Bridlewood, and Zephyr Heights. iOS Sep 2022, Android Nov 2022.
media.notes: G5. Still available on app stores.

media.title: My Little Pony: Secrets of Starlight
media.description: G5 Netflix special. Released alongside final Make Your Mark episodes. Closes Misty Brightdawn's arc.
media.notes: G5. Watch after Make Your Mark S5 finale.

media.title: My Little Pony: A Zephyr Heights Mystery
media.description: G5 action-adventure. Mane 5 investigate magic anomalies at the Zephyr Heights Gala. Up to 2-player co-op.
media.notes: G5. Final G5 retail game. PC via Sunshine streaming.

media.title: My Little Pony (Marvel/Star Comics)
media.description: G1 comic series published by Marvel's Star Comics imprint. 220+ issues over the full run, with six distinct sub-series. Among the most successful licensed comics of the 1980s despite its obscurity today.
media.notes: G1. Published 1985–1988 under Star Comics (Marvel). Multiple titled runs: My Little Pony (1985), My Little Pony in Merry Mirrorland (1986), and others. Out of print; no digital edition. Creators largely uncredited — only a handful identified as of 2022.

media.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic (IDW Comics)
media.description: G4 IDW Publishing ongoing series. 102 main issues plus numerous annuals, micro-series, and specials. Ran from November 2012 to September 2021.
media.notes: G4. Multiple creative teams including Katie Cook, Andy Price, Jeremy Whitley, and others. Supplemented by: My Little Pony Micro-Series (10 issues), Friends Forever (38 issues), Legends of Magic (12 issues), Feats of Friendship (3 issues), and annuals. Replaced by My Little Pony: Generations in Oct 2021.

media.title: My Little Pony: Equestria Girls (IDW Comics)
media.description: G4 Equestria Girls tie-in comic series from IDW. Multiple limited series and annuals running alongside the film franchise.
media.notes: G4 EG spin-off. Includes Holiday Special (2014), Annual 2015, and Digital Exclusives. Ran concurrently with EG film series.

media.title: My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic — Chapter Books & Novel Series
media.description: G4 prose fiction line published by Little, Brown. Includes the main Chapter Books series (character-focused novels), the Junior Novelization of the 2017 film, and the longer Ponyville Mysteries and Beyond Equestria novel series.
media.notes: G4. Published by Little, Brown and Company. Chapter Book series: ~17 volumes (2013–2016) covering Twilight Sparkle, Rainbow Dash, Fluttershy, Applejack, Rarity, and Pinkie Pie. Ponyville Mysteries: 6 volumes (2017–2018). Beyond Equestria: 3 volumes (2017–2018). All middle-grade prose, non-canon but officially licensed.
